content.problemStatement:
For an array BBB consisting of non-negative integers, let g(B,x)g(B, x)g(B,x) denote the maximum possible MEX†{MEX}^\dagMEX† of the array BBB we can obtain, by appending xxx integers to BBB.

For example, g([1,2,4],1)=3g([1, 2, 4], 1) = 3g([1,2,4],1)=3 as we can obtain the array [1,2,4,0][1, 2, 4, 0][1,2,4,0] by appending 000 which results in MEX=3MEX = 3MEX=3.

It can be shown that there exists a non-negative integer CCC such that g(B,x+1)−g(B,x)=1g(B, x + 1) - g(B, x) = 1g(B,x+1)−g(B,x)=1 for all x≥Cx \ge Cx≥C. We define f(B)f(B)f(B) as the **minimum** such CCC.

Given an array AAA of size NNN, find ∑L=1N∑R=LNf(A[L,R])\displaystyle{\sum_{L = 1}^{N}\sum_{R = L}^{N} f(A[L,R])}L=1∑N​R=L∑N​f(A[L,R]), where A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] represents the subarray [AL,AL+1,…,AR][A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots, A_R][AL​,AL+1​,…,AR​].

†MEX{ }^\dag MEX†MEX is the minimum excluded element, i.e., the minimum non-negative integer that does not appear in the array. For example, the MEXMEXMEX of [2,0,1,4][2, 0, 1, 4][2,0,1,4] is 333 and the MEXMEXMEX of [1][1][1] is 000.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the required sum.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
